2 Pricing Overview
interest Confidence 0.85

That's actually meaningful for a solo founder."

4 FAQ — Technical Questions
interest Confidence 0.85

5 FAQ Answers — Too Shallow
annoyance Confidence 0.80

"'Both! You can choose once you get access' — cool, supports both. But man, these FAQ answers are copy-paste level thin. A dev wants to know — is it fully typed? Strict mode? Server components? The marketing is aimed at devs but the FAQ treats us like we don't know what we're asking. '/app router or /pages router?' — exact same answer: 'Both! You can choose once you get access.' Same generic answer for two different technical questions. Alright, let me check the demo — I want to see actual code quality."

⚠ Copy-paste FAQ answers for technical questions undermine developer trust

11 Final Purchase Decision
anticipation Confidence 0.95

"Decision time. Starter at $199 is a trap — no lifetime updates, no community. All-in at $249 is the sweet spot — lifetime updates mean I get value every time Marc pushes a new feature. The Bundle at $299 adds CodeFast which I don't need since I already know how to code. $249 across every future project amortizes to basically nothing. I'd click 'Get ShipFast' on the All-in tier right now."

✓ Decided on All-in — lifetime updates and community access justify the $50 premium
